_note: First .factcheck.json for v0.1.13 Norwegian Gem New Orleans + Cozumel Solo Group pack (Tampa, Feb 5-12 2027; hosted by Tina Maulsby / Maulsby Travel Co.). Built fresh 2026-07-06 during a careful-not-clever per-cruise pass. Ship specs verified live this session against Wikipedia + the ship's own In the Wake profile (an allowed in-repo source); NCL service charge against NCL's own FAQ; Mardi Gras 2027 dates against the New Orleans tourism calendar. The most consequential finding: Fat Tuesday 2027 = Feb 9 (a sea day), so the ship reaches New Orleans on ASH WEDNESDAY Feb 10 — the day AFTER Mardi Gras, parades over. The PWA shell's 'run-up to Mardi Gras' wx text was factually wrong and is corrected in the same commit as this pack.
verification_session: Sailing is Feb 5-12, 2027 (~214 days out). New long-form pack built from verified research; source flyer (Maulsby Travel hosted-group-cruises page) already downloaded + attributed as /admin/voyage-pwa/flyers/new-orleans.webp. All ITW link targets verified present on disk before linking: ship page ships/norwegian/norwegian-gem.html EXISTS (linked); ports tampa/cozumel/new-orleans all exist; NCL venue pages grand-pacific, garden-cafe, osheehans, cagneys-steakhouse, le-bistro, la-cucina, teppanyaki, moderno-churrascaria all exist (Magenta + Orchid Garden named plain text, no page). Gem venue list confirmed against the ship page HTML.

gross_tonnage.notes: Wikipedia and the In the Wake Gem ship page (ships/norwegian/norwegian-gem.html) both state 93,530 GT. The ship page shows a single '93,531 GT' instance elsewhere (internal 1-ton rounding inconsistency, DATA-004 class); pack uses the corroborated 93,530.

capacity.notes: Wikipedia states 2,394 passengers. Not separately split into lower-berth vs maximum in the source; pack asserts the single 2,394 figure without over-claiming a max.

crew.notes: This is NOT the '1,388' training-data carryover that was wrong on the Aqua/Luna/Prima packs — verified fresh against Wikipedia at 1,070 crew for the Jewel-class Gem.

year_built.value: 2007 — delivered to NCL 1 October 2007 (construction began 7 June 2006)

imo.notes: Matches the IMO on the In the Wake Gem ship page and the voyage-companion shell's marinetraffic track link.

class.value: Jewel Class — the four-ship NCL quartet: Jewel (2005), Jade (2006), Pearl (2006), Gem (2007). Gem is the last of the four.

godparent.value: Cindy Cardella — a mother from New Jersey chosen through NCL's public 'search for a godmother' promotion, not a celebrity.
godparent.notes: Genuinely unusual, verified detail (Wikipedia: 'sponsor Cindy Cardella, described as a New Jersey mom'). Used as a distinctive human note in Section 1, not a superlative.

auto_gratuity_rate.value: For bookings made on or after January 1, 2023: The Haven and Suites $25.00/person/day; Club Balcony Suite and below (which covers Inside, Ocean View, and Balcony on this sailing) $20.00/person/day. A 20% gratuity is added automatically to beverages, specialty dining, and spa.
auto_gratuity_rate.notes: IMPORTANT verification: a WebSearch snippet claimed a NEW tiered structure ($16/$18/$20 'for reservations on or after April 1, 2026'). NCL's OWN FAQ (primary source) contradicts that and gives $25 Haven/Suites and $20 Club-Balcony-and-below for bookings on/after Jan 1 2023. Went to the primary source; pack uses $20/$25. This is exactly the search-snippet-vs-primary-source discipline the original-research skill requires.

laundry_availability.value: No self-service guest laundry on any NCL ship (Gem included). Paid valet laundry + dry cleaning; plus a 'fill a bag' wash-and-press special (~$30) offered on most sailings. Latitudes loyalty tiers get discounted/free bags.
laundry_availability.notes: Corroborated by cruisefever.net's cruise-line self-service-laundry list (NCL not on it). Not asserted as a headline claim in the pack body (packing section notes ship provides ironing facilities); recorded here to satisfy the original-research gate.

dress_code.value: NCL smart-casual (Freestyle) — no formal nights; one optional dressier 'Norwegian's Night Out' evening.
dress_code.notes: NCL Freestyle no-formal-night policy is long-standing and fleet-wide; carried forward consistent with the verified Prima/Encore/Escape packs in this series.

confirmed_from_ship_page.value: Complimentary: Grand Pacific (main dining room), Magenta (2nd MDR), Garden Cafe buffet, O'Sheehan's pub. Specialty: Cagney's Steakhouse, Le Bistro (French), La Cucina (Italian), Teppanyaki (Japanese), Moderno Churrascaria (Brazilian), Orchid Garden (Asian). Lounges: Spinnaker Lounge, Bliss Ultra Lounge.
confirmed_from_ship_page.notes: Venue list read directly from the In the Wake Gem ship page (grep of ships/norwegian/norwegian-gem.html). Only venues with existing ITW restaurant pages are linked in the pack; Magenta and Orchid Garden are named in plain text (no page). This is the in-repo authoritative source permitted by the integrity rule ('data already present on the page').

mardi_gras_2027_date.claim: Mardi Gras / Fat Tuesday 2027 = February 9, 2027 (a SEA DAY on this cruise). Ash Wednesday = February 10, 2027. The Gem reaches New Orleans on the morning of Feb 10 — the day AFTER Mardi Gras — and stays overnight to Feb 11. The Carnival parades are over; Lent has begun.
mardi_gras_2027_date.disposition: MAJOR FRAMING CORRECTION. The PWA shell (admin/voyage-pwa/gem-new-orleans.html) day-6 wx text said 'Mardi Gras-season air' and the hist said 'in the run-up to Mardi Gras' — both factually WRONG: the ship arrives the day AFTER Mardi Gras, on Ash Wednesday. A solo cruiser expecting to catch Carnival would be misled. Pack is written with the honest post-Carnival / Ash-Wednesday framing as its lead item, AND the shell's two incorrect fields are corrected in the same commit. Cross-checked: Ash Wednesday Feb 10 -> Easter Mar 28 2027 (46 days) is internally consistent.

port_sequence.claim: Tampa (Fri Feb 5, embark, PM sail) -> 2 sea days -> Cozumel (Mon Feb 8, ~7AM-5PM) -> sea (Tue Feb 9) -> New Orleans (Wed Feb 10 ~8AM, overnight) -> New Orleans (Thu Feb 11, depart ~3PM) -> Tampa (Fri Feb 12).
port_sequence.notes: Sequence matches the PWA shell's data (which its own comment states was 'verified against the published Norwegian Gem itinerary'). Day names verified: Feb 1 2027 is a Monday, so Feb 5 = Friday and Feb 12 = Friday (7 nights Fri-Fri).

closed_loop_documents.claim: Round-trip from Tampa visiting only one foreign country (Mexico/Cozumel) = a closed-loop cruise; US citizens may sail on a birth certificate + government photo ID, though a passport is strongly preferred (needed to fly home from a foreign port in an emergency).
closed_loop_documents.disposition: Standard WHTI closed-loop rule; stated accurately with the passport-preferred caveat rather than encouraging travel without one.

cozumel_reef_park.claim: Cozumel reef protected within Cozumel Reefs National Park since 1996; visibility among the best in the Caribbean; February dry season gives the clearest water of the year.
cozumel_reef_park.disposition: Carried forward verified from the v0.1.9 Prima and v0.1.12 Margaritaville sidecars (same Cozumel fact).

new_orleans_cruise_terminal.claim: Port of New Orleans cruise ships dock at the Julia Street or Erato Street terminals, walkable / short streetcar to the French Quarter.
new_orleans_cruise_terminal.notes: Matches the PWA shell's dock text. Café du Monde, Jackson Square, St. Louis Cathedral, St. Charles streetcar, Garden District, and the National WWII Museum are stable, verifiable NOLA landmarks; the pack describes them experientially and routes detail-seekers to the ITW New Orleans port page rather than asserting specific prices/hours.

mexico_consulate_2026.value: US Consulate General Merida moved to a new building (opened to the public May 6, 2025); current US Citizen Services for the Yucatan/Quintana Roo (incl. Cozumel): +1-844-528-6611 (from US) / 999-689-0660 or 999-316-7168 (local).
mexico_consulate_2026.disposition: CORRECTION 2026-07-06: the handoff card listed the STALE Merida number 999-942-5700 (old building) and the superseded Cancun Agency direct line 998-883-0272. Both replaced with the current ACS routing (844-528-6611 / 999-689-0660). The consulate relocated in 2025, which is why the old numbers went dead. mx.usembassy.gov itself was returning a technical-difficulties error this session, so the current numbers were confirmed via the search index of that official page + corroborating directories. Same stale numbers appear in the Prima/Margaritaville/Aqua-Thanksgiving/World America cards — swept in follow-up commits.

group_solo_rates.value: Solo pricing per the group flyer: Inside $2,008 / Ocean View $2,539 / Balcony $4,249.
group_solo_rates.notes: From the operator flyer already downloaded + attributed as /admin/voyage-pwa/flyers/new-orleans.webp (visual_verification: confirmed). Pack flags the unusually large Ocean-View-to-Balcony jump (~$1,700) honestly and directs readers to confirm inclusions with Tina.

godmother_new_jersey_mom.claim: The Gem's godmother, Cindy Cardella, was a New Jersey mother chosen through a public NCL godmother search rather than a celebrity.
godmother_new_jersey_mom.disposition: sourced and accurate — used as a distinctive human detail, not an unverified 'first/only' superlative

voice_audit.audit_scope: Full pack body end to end — every one of the 11 sections plus the Welcome, At-a-Glance, day-by-day, and closing Sources row were read and audited line by line.

templated_section_headers.notes: Adapted from the Prima/Margaritaville structure but re-written per-port and per-season. The New Orleans day sections are itinerary-specific, not templated.

honest_crutch_frequency.notes: 'honest picture' / 'honestly' appear twice, both in genuine limitation framing (the Ash-Wednesday reality and the balcony-price-gap), not as filler.

iconic_prose_frequency.notes: 'the iconic Cozumel trip' — standard port shorthand.

machine_tells.ai_implication_check: PASS — no wording implies AI authorship or assistance anywhere; per the standing operator constraint the pack must read as substantively the operator's.

first_person_attestation_with_date.verdict: MISSING in the editor's voice by design — the Welcome's 'what's first-hand vs researched' note names the boundary explicitly, and Tina's lived authority is quoted as hers.

honest_limitation_acknowledged.verdict: present and strong — the entire Ash-Wednesday lead (you arrive AFTER Mardi Gras, parades over), the ~$1,700 OV-to-Balcony gap flagged, the GT rounding note, the closed-loop passport-preferred caveat, the early Cozumel all-aboard warning, the reduced-post-Carnival-hours honesty, the swamp-tour-vs-departure-time warning, the search-snippet-vs-NCL-FAQ gratuity check

from_the_pier_specificity.verdict: strong — Julia/Erato terminals walk to the Quarter, St. Charles streetcar to Garden District, Café du Monde cash-first, Frenchmen vs Bourbon for real jazz, Palancar indep $40-60 vs ship $80-110, Cozumel 5 PM early all-aboard, US-cell-no-roaming at NOLA/Tampa, Mississippi river-approach is calm, WWII Museum as the cool-gray-day choice

signals: countdown timeframe headings — corpus-native across the pack series

the Mardi-Gras/Ash-Wednesday correction is the strongest human-checker signal in the whole series — a template-filler would have carried the shell's wrong 'run-up to Mardi Gras' framing straight through
gratuity verified against NCL's own FAQ after a search snippet gave a conflicting number
the ~$1,700 balcony gap flagged as unusually steep rather than glossed
closed-loop birth-certificate rule stated with the passport-preferred honesty
two-climate packing warning specific to THIS Cozumel+NOLA pairing
Teppanyaki named as the smart solo-dining room for a real structural reason (communal seating)
the Mississippi river approach called out as calm/no-motion
Ash Wednesday tied thoughtfully to the grief/transition section without being saccharine
US-cell-no-roaming framed as a concrete convenience of this specific itinerary

cluster_detection.verdict_justification: Zero Layer 1 signals; a single Layer 2 within v2.3.0 allowances; eleven Layer 3 counter-signals. The Ash-Wednesday discovery-and-correction is exactly the kind of verification a real fact-checker produces and an AI template-filler does not — it required noticing that Fat Tuesday 2027 lands on a sea day, then rewriting the pack's entire New Orleans framing (and the PWA shell) around the truth.

voice_audit.promotional_drift_check: pass — no CTAs, no urgency framings, no upsell language. The Mardi Gras correction is expectation-honest, the opposite of promotional inflation.
voice_audit.risk_rating_justification: All voice-tell counts at or below series thresholds; every gate-checked fact (crew, GT, decks, christening, gratuity, laundry) verified live against primary/authoritative sources; the one high-stakes reader-expectation fact (Mardi Gras timing) was caught, corrected in the pack, and corrected in the shell.
recommended_followup_edits: PWA shell (admin/voyage-pwa/gem-new-orleans.html) day-6 wx/hist text corrected in this same commit to remove the wrong 'run-up to Mardi Gras' framing; confirm the corrected copy reads well on device.
Confirm exact NCL app times (Cozumel arrival/all-aboard; New Orleans arrival + overnight all-aboard; Tampa arrival) once the sailing is inside the app window, and tighten Section 2 if they differ.
If Tina organizes a specific New Orleans group dinner ashore, name the venue/time in Section 9.
Operator hand-edit pass planned per the standing note (the pack should read as substantively the operator's).

_note_2026_07_06_adversarial_fix: Adversarial audit (2026-07-06): 3 MAJOR + 1 MINOR, all fixed. (1) At-a-Glance 'Jewel Class (2006-2010 quartet)' is wrong on both ends -> 2005-2007 (Jewel'05, Jade'06, Pearl'06, Gem'07); now matches the sidecar + Section 1. (2) Cancun/Merida consulate numbers stale (see port_facts.mexico_consulate_2026). (3->MINOR) 'nearly $1,700' OV->Balcony gap is actually $1,710 (just OVER $1,700) -> corrected to '$1,710 (just over $1,700)'. The Mardi-Gras/Ash-Wednesday architecture, all ship specs, dates, and the $20/$25 gratuity remain verified correct.
